description: §26.8.2 / §26.8.3 — a multi-rule utility (`.z { @apply prose; }`, the §26.6 typography family) whose registry output is more than one flat rule fires E-APPLY-NON-INLINABLE-UTILITY (Error). Flat-inlining a non-single-rule utility would drop its selector structure. `prose` is RECOGNIZED (not unknown) but non-inlinable.
rationale: §26.8.2: `prose` (a §26.6 multi-rule / pseudo-element-bearing family) cannot be flat-inlined into the enclosing rule → E-APPLY-NON-INLINABLE-UTILITY (Error, §26.8.3), anchored at the `@apply` span. It is classified NON-INLINABLE, not UNKNOWN — `prose` DOES resolve in the embedded registry, but its output is more than one flat `.prose { … }` rule, so inlining it flat would apply its declarations to the wrong element. Resolution: use the utility directly in a `class=` attribute.
